content.text:
<user_query>
add this to the cursor rules: when running node always run from /Users/duhl/.local/share/mise/shims/node
</user_query>

Checking how Cursor rules are structured in this repo so we can add the Node path rule.

[REDACTED]

Adding the rule to `toolchain.mdc` — it already covers Node/mise and is always applied.

[REDACTED]

input.old_string: - When running shell commands, scripts, or tests, ensure the environment uses mise’s toolchain (e.g. activated mise shell integration, or `mise exec -- <command>` so the correct Node version is on `PATH`).
input.new_string:
- When running shell commands, scripts, or tests, ensure the environment uses mise’s toolchain (e.g. activated mise shell integration, or `mise exec -- <command>` so the correct Node version is on `PATH`).
- **When invoking `node` directly** (e.g. in shell commands the agent runs), use the shim at `/Users/duhl/.local/share/mise/shims/node` so the pinned toolchain is used even when `PATH` is minimal.

content.text:
Here’s what changed.

**Update:** `.cursor/rules/toolchain.mdc` — under **Node and package managers**, added:

- **When invoking `node` directly** (e.g. in shell commands the agent runs), use the shim at `/Users/duhl/.local/share/mise/shims/node` so the pinned toolchain is used even when `PATH` is minimal.

It sits with the existing mise guidance and keeps `alwaysApply: true`, so it applies to every chat in this workspace.

**Note:** That path is tied to your machine (`duhl`). Anyone else cloning the repo would use their own mise shim path unless you later switch to something like `$(mise which node)` in docs or shared scripts. If you want this rule only for you, keeping it in your user-level Cursor rules instead of the repo is another option.

[REDACTED]
